news.title: About 1.6 lakh notices issued to property tax defaulters by civic bodies in Telangana
news.para: Civic authorities in Hyderabad's Core Urban Region (CURE) are intensifying efforts to recover pending property taxes, issuing over 1.64 lakh notices to defaulters. With 40% of property owners yet to pay, a significant Rs 4,201 crore remains outstanding against a total demand of Rs 6,387 crore. A One-Time Settlement Scheme offering a 90% interest rebate is available until March 31.

news.title: Haryana eyes Ambala’s expansion with 6,600-acre land buyback
news.para: Haryana government is acquiring over 6,600 acres for Ambala's urban expansion through a digital, consent-based process. Farmers in seven villages can voluntarily sell land via the e-Bhoomi portal, setting their own prices. This initiative aims to build new residential and commercial hubs with modern infrastructure, avoiding traditional acquisition disputes.

news.title: Panel to look into property tax issues from merged areas of Pune
news.para: Pune Mayor Manjusha Nagpure has announced a committee to address property tax concerns in merged areas after citizens complained of high levies. The state government had previously suspended tax collection due to these complaints. The committee will consult with representatives and seek state guidance on tax structures and infrastructure development.

news.title: RSVM Hospitality buys 18.6-acre land parcel in Thane for ₹498 crore
news.para: RSVM Hospitality has secured a significant land deal in Thane. The company acquired over 18.6 acres for more than Rs 498 crore from Neterwala Group entities. This large transaction occurred in Majiwada, Thane district. Such acquisitions are fueling future residential and mixed-use developments in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region. Thane's real estate market is experiencing strong growth.

news.title: Noida: Mahagun Mywoods runs on genset for 11 hours after NPCL cuts power
news.para: Power supply to Mahagun Mywoods in Greater Noida West was cut for over 11 hours on Monday due to the developer's failure to clear electricity dues of approximately Rs 72 lakh. Thousands of residents were left relying on diesel generators until the connection was restored in the evening after partial payment.

news.title: MahaRERA resolves 6,945 homebuyer complaints in a year; focus on clearing backlog
news.para: MahaRERA resolved a record 6,945 homebuyer complaints last year, significantly improving its disposal rate. While thousands of cases remain, hearings are underway for most. The authority is prioritizing older disputes and enhancing project scrutiny to protect buyers and ensure timely project completion, despite challenges in recovering dues from developers.

news.title: Ensure lifts, ramps for disabled in residential complexes: Allahabad HC
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has mandated that residential complexes in Uttar Pradesh must offer accessible parking and facilities like lifts and gyms for persons with disabilities. This ruling emphasizes that accessibility is a fundamental right within community living spaces. The court stressed that builders must adhere to disability-friendly guidelines during building approvals and before issuing completion certificates.

news.title: Bombay HC dismisses plea by Koli community societies challenging slum redevelopment in Khar West
news.para: Bombay High Court dismissed a petition by Koli community societies challenging a committee's report on slum redevelopment. The court stated that the dispute over land demarcation and its impact on traditional fishing activities requires a civil court for resolution, not writ jurisdiction. The status quo was maintained for three weeks.

news.title: Janus Living eyes $5 billion valuation in US IPO
news.para: Senior housing REIT Janus Living is targeting a valuation of up to $5 billion in its U.S. IPO, seeking to raise $740 million. Despite market volatility, the company, carved out from Healthpeak Properties, is proceeding with its listing. Janus owns 34 senior housing communities, primarily in Florida and Texas, catering to rising demand driven by aging demographics.

news.title: Lucknow civic body to charge 12% interest on house tax defaulters
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation will levy a 12% interest on house tax defaulters after March 31, with strict actions planned for continued non-payment. An awareness drive was conducted in Zone-3, and all zonal offices will operate extended hours, including during Navratri and Eid, to facilitate timely tax deposits.

news.title: Punjab aims to develop Mohali as tier-I information technology hub
news.para: Punjab is positioning itself as a prime destination for IT investment and digital innovation, with plans to develop Mohali into a Tier 1 IT hub. The state is offering robust fiscal and non-fiscal incentives under its Industrial and Business Development Policy 2026 to attract IT and ITeS units, including significant SGST reimbursement and employment generation subsidies.

news.title: NCLAT permits Chronos Properties to renew bank guarantee, valid for next six months
news.para: The NCLAT has allowed Brookfield-backed Chronos Properties to replace its expired bank guarantee within a week for the IL&FS Bandra Kurla Complex property. The tribunal also extended its interim order preventing IL&FS from creating third-party rights on its headquarters. Chronos' bid was initially Rs 1,080 crore, later revised by IL&FS to Rs 1,481 crore.

news.title: DRA Homes acquires 3.91 acres land in Chennai for ₹49.70 crore
news.para: DRA Homes has purchased 3.91 acres of land in Karanai, Chennai. The cost of the land was ₹49.70 crore. The company intends to build a mid-segment residential community on this site. The estimated value of the homes to be developed is around ₹500 crore. This acquisition strengthens DRA Homes' development plans in Chennai.

news.title: Manglam Group to invest ₹400 crore to develop commercial project in Jaipur
news.para: Manglam Group is set to invest around ₹400 crore in developing Summit Signature Sangam, a commercial project on Ajmer Road, Jaipur. In partnership with Sangam India, the development will span over 11,500 sq yards, featuring a ground + three floor structure with floor plates commencing at 1,100 sq ft.

news.title: ED raids residential, office premises of Mantri Developers, directors in Bengaluru
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) on Monday carried out search operations at the residential and office premises of real estate firm Mantri Developers Private Limited and its directors in Bengaluru in connection with a money laundering investigation, officials said.

news.title: FractoProp receives SEBI registration for Ridhama Real Estate Fund
news.para: FractoProp has secured SEBI registration for its Ridhama Real Estate Fund, a Category II AIF. Sponsored by R.D. Brothers Group, the fund aims for a ₹50 crore corpus, with a potential ₹125 crore size including a greenshoe option. It plans to invest ₹10-12 crore per project.

news.title: Now, request construction waste pick-up online as Delhi civic body rolls out portal
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi has launched an online portal to manage construction and demolition waste, aiming to curb illegal dumping and promote recycling. Residents can request waste collection, connecting them with empanelled transporters for efficient disposal and resource recovery.

news.title: Brick by brick, ‘coal mafia’ chokes kiln industry in Ludhiana
news.para: Brick prices have surged over 5% in Ludhiana, with a single brick now costing Rs 80, due to a dramatic increase in coal prices. Industry leaders blame a "coal mafia" for manipulating supply chains, leading to nearly doubled coal costs.  Hundreds of brick kilns threaten to shut down by April 1 if governments do not intervene against these "arbitrary practices."

news.title: Pune: Hinjewadi-Shivajinagar metro line to miss deadline yet again
news.para: The 23-km Hinjewadi-Shivajinagar Metro corridor, 94% complete, is unlikely to meet its March-end target. Full passenger services are now anticipated by June or July, pending operational readiness confirmation from the concessionaire and necessary approvals. Officials suggest a full route launch with some stations temporarily closed is more viable than phased operations.

news.title: China's new home prices extend decline in February as recovery remains elusive
news.para: China's property market shows continued price drops in new homes. February saw a 3.2% annual decline, worse than January. Major cities like Beijing and Shanghai report slight gains, but lower-tier cities struggle. The sector remains a drag on the economy. Policymakers aim to stabilize the market without returning to old debt-fueled growth models.
